content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Dale P. Borden 
348 Chestnut Street 
Vacaville, CA 
95688 
Dear Mr. Borden: 
September 27, 1989 
Re: 
Our File No. G-89-556 
We have received your letter, dated September 21, 1989, 
concerning a possible violation of the conflict of interest 
provisions of the Political Reform Act! by public officials on the 
Vacaville School Board, Library Commission and City Council. 
Enclosed are a pamphlet explaining the conflict of interest laws 
and a complaint form. 
The Commission does not provide advice concerning past 
conduct of public officials; furthermore, we only provide advice 
to public officials and their authorized representatives. 
Complaints about possible violations of the Political Reform Act 
are handled by our Enforcement Division. 
After you review the 
enclosed pamphlet, you may wish to file a complaint with the 
Enforcement Division concerning possible conflict of interest 
violations. 
If you have any questions about filing a complaint, 
please contact the Enforcement Division at (916) 322-6441. 
Sincerely, 
~! 
L\ 
Kathryn ~. Donovan 
General Counsel 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916)322,5660

sections.question_synthetic: The sender inquired about possible conflict of interest violations by public officials on the Vacaville School Board, Library Commission, and City Council.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC does not provide advice on past conduct of public officials and only advises public officials and their authorized representatives; the sender should file a complaint with the Enforcement Division if they wish to pursue the matter.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. This is a declination letter explaining why the FPPC cannot provide the requested advice. The sender appears to be a private citizen rather than a public official seeking advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The sender inquired about possible conflict of interest violations by public officials on the Vacaville School Board, Library Commission, and City Council.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC does not provide advice on past conduct of public officials and only advises public officials and their authorized representatives; the sender should file a complaint with the Enforcement Division if they wish to pursue the matter.

embedding.summary: This is a response letter from the FPPC General Counsel declining to provide advice on a complaint about possible conflict of interest violations by Vacaville public officials, directing the sender to file a formal complaint with the Enforcement Division instead.
